report_name: Chinese APT Mustang Panda Targets Indian Banks, Korean Policy Experts in Espionage Campaign

threat_actors.name: Mustang Panda
threat_actors.description: A Chinese state-sponsored cyber-espionage group conducting a campaign targeting the Indian financial sector and public policy experts in Korea and the U.S. The group uses spear-phishing and DLL side-loading to deploy the LotusLite backdoor for intelligence gathering.
threat_actors.goals: Intelligence gathering aligned with Beijing's geopolitical interests.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Attachment
attack_patterns.description: Used as the initial access vector, delivering malicious files like ZIP archives with LNK files to victims via email.

attack_patterns.name: Hijack Execution Flow
attack_patterns.description: The core execution technique used by Mustang Panda, where a legitimate, signed application is tricked into loading a malicious DLL placed in the same directory.

attack_patterns.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution
attack_patterns.description: The LotusLite backdoor establishes persistence by creating or modifying a Windows Registry key in a 'Run' key to ensure it executes on system startup.

attack_patterns.name: Application Layer Protocol
attack_patterns.description: The LotusLite backdoor uses web protocols to connect to its command and control server for remote command execution and file transfer.

attack_patterns.name: Windows Command Shell
attack_patterns.description: The LotusLite backdoor provides the attacker with a remote shell to execute commands on the compromised system.

attack_patterns.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
attack_patterns.description: The malware used decoys, such as displaying a pop-up with 'HDFC Bank' branding, to hide its true purpose and allay victim suspicion.

malware.name: LotusLite
malware.description: A custom backdoor deployed by Mustang Panda via DLL side-loading. It enables remote command execution, file access, and intelligence gathering from compromised systems.

identities.name: HDFC Bank
identities.description: An Indian private-sector bank whose branding was used as a decoy by Mustang Panda's malware to deceive victims in the Indian financial sector.

identities.name: Victor Cha
identities.description: An American political scientist who was impersonated by Mustang Panda using a Google account to add legitimacy to spear-phishing outreach targeting policy experts.

infrastructure.name: LotusLite C2 Server
infrastructure.description: Attacker-controlled command and control server used by the LotusLite backdoor for remote command execution and file access.

indicators.name: Mustang Panda DLL Side-Loading Detection
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential DLL Side-Loading by Legitimate Process
id: 2a9b3c4d-5e6f-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects a legitimate, signed process loading a DLL from a non-standard, user-writable directory, a technique commonly used by Mustang Panda.
references:
  - https://www.acronis.com/en-us/blog/posts/mustang-panda-attack-chain
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-21
tags:
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: medium
logsource:
  category: image_load
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_process:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\Acrobat.exe'
      - '\WINWORD.EXE'
      - '\EXCEL.EXE'
      - '\POWERPNT.EXE'
  selection_path:
    ImageLoaded|startswith:
      - 'C:\Users\'
      - 'C:\ProgramData\'
      - 'C:\Temp\'
      - 'C:\Windows\Temp\'
  condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate applications that incorrectly package their DLLs.
  - Portable applications running from user directories.
indicators.description: Detects the DLL side-loading technique used by Mustang Panda where a legitimate application loads a malicious DLL from a non-standard directory.

indicators.name: Mustang Panda Registry Run Key Persistence
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Suspicious Modification of HKCU Run Key
id: 1b8c2d3e-4f5a-6b7c-8d9e-0f1a2b3c4d5e
status: experimental
description: Detects modification of the HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run registry key, a common persistence technique used by malware like LotusLite.
references:
  - https://www.acronis.com/en-us/blog/posts/mustang-panda-attack-chain
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-21
tags:
  - attack.t1547.001
  - attack.persistence
level: medium
logsource:
  category: registry_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    EventType: 'SetValue'
    TargetObject|contains: '\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate software installers adding autostart entries.
indicators.description: Detects the creation of persistence mechanisms in the Windows Registry Run key, as used by LotusLite.

relationships.source_name: Mustang Panda Registry Run Key Persistence
Mustang Panda Registry Run Key Persistence
